title: Reading and Use of English – Part 7
description: You are going to read an article from which six paragraphs have been removed. Choose from paragraphs A–G which fits each gap. There is one extra paragraph you do not need to use.

content.text: Bicycles have become an integral part of urban life today. However, their journey from invention to widespread use was not straightforward. The earliest known bicycle design appeared around the early 19th century. [41] The initial models were rather cumbersome and difficult to ride. As the design improved, so did public interest. [42] This period saw significant technological advancements in bicycle manufacturing, leading to the formation of cycling clubs. || By the late 19th century, the so-called 'safety bicycle' became popular due to its chain-driven mechanism. [43] In the following decades, bicycles evolved both technologically and culturally. They were no longer just a mode of transport but a symbol of freedom. [44] As automobiles became more accessible in the 20th century, the bicycle's popularity faced a challenge. Environmental and health concerns in recent years have revived interest in cycling. [45] Despite modern developments, the core structure of bicycles has remained largely unchanged. This blend of tradition and innovation is what keeps bicycles relevant today. [46]

paragraphs.A: At this point, inventors began experimenting with different materials, such as metal, to increase durability and reduce weight.
paragraphs.B: Nevertheless, many people still view them as the most efficient way to navigate crowded cityscapes.
paragraphs.C: This discovery spurred widespread enthusiasm, as it addressed many safety and stability concerns.
paragraphs.D: In contrast, this new model boasted increased efficiency and comfort, making cycling accessible to a wider audience.
paragraphs.E: Furthermore, it encouraged women to engage in cycling, a pursuit that was previously considered impractical.
paragraphs.F: As a result, many cycling competitions were organized, further promoting the sport.
paragraphs.G: The automobile industry also experienced a renaissance around this time, with innovations that captivated a global audience.

questions.explanation: Paragraph A contains 'At this point,' which indicates a next phase or step after early designs referenced before gap 41.
F uses 'As a result,' which logically follows the mention of technological advancements leading to organized competitions.
C mentions 'This discovery,' directly referring to the 'safety bicycle' introduced just before the gap.
E starts with 'Furthermore,' adding information about societal changes, linking back to the cultural impact mentioned before the gap.
B starts with 'Nevertheless,' implying a contrast with automobiles' popularity, which was discussed right before the gap.
D contains 'In contrast,' which refers back to the previously mentioned balance of tradition and innovation.
